description: The project schema defines the structure of a Swallow project that can be used by the Swallow Pricing Engine. Swallow https://swallow.app.

meta.description: Meta information about the project. This is used to identify the project and its version.

description.description: The description of the project version. e.g. what changes happened.

summary.description: A detailed summary of the model. This is generated by an LLM.

project_reference.description: A unique reference UUID for this project.

tests.description: Defined tests for the project that specify the expected input and output results.

items.description: The project test schema defines the tests that can be run against the project. Each test has a unique id, a key, a name, input data, mock data, tags, and an expected output.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

name.description: The name of the project test.

mocks.description: The test mock inputs to override external calls.

modules.description: Included sub-projects. They can be accessed by the modules step

items.description: This is a project within a project. It is used in module steps.

project_reference.description: A unique reference UUID for this project.

version_reference.description: A unique reference UUID for this version.

project.description: The project details of the included module

packages.description: The external packages that Swallow uses as extensions to javascript for expressions and custom code

mathjs.description: Full documentation for the Math.js library can be found at https://mathjs.org/docs/index.html
mathjs.example: Math.js is a powerful library for mathematics in JavaScript. It provides a wide range of functions for arithmetic, algebra, calculus, and more.

lodash.description: Full documentation for the Lodash library can be found at https://lodash.com/docs/4.17.15
lodash.example: Lodash is a modern JavaScript utility library delivering modularity, performance, and extras.

1liner.example: 1liner is a library that allows you to write concise one line queries to transform objects and arrays in to values.

input.description: The input json schema for the project. This defines the initial payload to be transformed into a quote price.

key.description: The key for the format

label.description: The summary text that describes this property

sub_label.description: The detailed explanation of the property. This can be HTML.

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to format the value. It can be used to transform the value into a different format or to apply a function to the value. The expression should return a value of the type specified in the type property.

type.description: The data type of the formatted value

def.description: The default value if the expression can not resolve or find a value

items.description: This is an array of possible values that can be used to build tests or web form UI options

index.description: Whether to index this format item so it can be found easier later. Limited to 4 format items.

static.description: Whether this is a static property that cannot be changed from quote inputs. i.e. tax rate or currency conversion rate

patternProperties.exp: This will normally be the same as the key with curly brackets i.e. {{key_name}}. It is possible to map to nested properties like {{key_name.child}}. This is a javascript expression that is used to format the value. It can be used to transform the value into a different format or to apply a function to the value. The expression should return a value of the type specified in the type property.

format_children.description: An optional transform of project initial payload to another object. i.e. a nested object with properties that can be transformed.

key.description: The key for the format

label.description: The summary text that describes this property

sub_label.description: The detailed explanation of the property. This can be HTML.

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to format the value. It can be used to transform the value into a different format or to apply a function to the value. The expression should return a value of the type specified in the type property.

type.description: The data type of the formatted value

def.description: The default value if the expression can not resolve or find a value

items.description: This is an array of possible values that can be used to build tests or web form UI options

index.description: Whether to index this format item so it can be found easier later. Limited to 4 format items.

static.description: Whether this is a static property that cannot be changed from quote inputs. i.e. tax rate or currency conversion rate

batch.description: Whether this is a batch input - i.e. an array of quote inputs are supplied which will be processed in either module or code steps.

mapping.description: Optional javascript code mapping that will transform the payload into input schema defined in format. This is evaluated in the context of the input payload.

oneOf.description: This step is used to transform inputs or step outputs in to new value that can be outputted to the next step. It can be used to aggregate, format, or manipulate data in various ways.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

retain.description: If we want the input to be returned with the output i.e. retain properties

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

key.description: The key for the format

label.description: The summary text that describes this property

sub_label.description: The detailed explanation of the property. This can be HTML.

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to format the value. It can be used to transform the value into a different format or to apply a function to the value. The expression should return a value of the type specified in the type property.

type.description: The data type of the formatted value

def.description: The default value if the expression can not resolve or find a value

items.description: This is an array of possible values that can be used to build tests or web form UI options

index.description: Whether to index this format item so it can be found easier later. Limited to 4 format items.

static.description: Whether this is a static property that cannot be changed from quote inputs. i.e. tax rate or currency conversion rate

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step allows for the definition of labels that can be applied to a quote. Labels are used to categorize or tag quotes with specific attributes.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

type.description: The data type of the label value

def.description: The default value it the expression can not resolve a value

name.description: The text for the form label for this property

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to evaluate a response value.

description.description: This can be used to provide a description or code

value.description: The label value when an expression condition is tripped

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is allows a large data set to map all linked values from a keyed value. e.g. a postcode lookup and enrichment. It does similar to collections step but less flexible

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

exp.description: The expression query to evaluate the link needle

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

label.description: The text for the form label for this linked property

type.description: The data type of the linked value

def.description: The default value the link will resolve too.

links.description: Mapped links to objects that extend the output. i.e. linking a car type to car weight and emissions

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is used to define a set of exclusions, as a boolean, that can be applied to a quote. An exclusion is flag that determines the validity of a quote.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

name.description: The text for the form label for this property

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to determine if the item should be excluded from the result. It can be used to filter out items based on their properties or values. The expression should return a boolean value.

description.description: This can be used to provide a description or code

def.description: The default value when an expression condition is not resolved

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is used to define a set of refer rules, as a boolean, that can be applied to a quote. An refer is a flag that can be used to request further validation or review of a quote.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

name.description: The text for the form label for this property

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to determine if the item should be excluded from the result. It can be used to filter out items based on their properties or values. The expression should return a boolean value.

description.description: This can be used to provide a description or code

def.description: The default value when an expression condition is not resolved

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is used to define excess or deductible, as a number, that can be applied to a quote.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

def.description: The default array of excesses if the expression can not resolve a value

name.description: The text for the form label for this property

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to evaluate a response value.

description.description: This can be used to provide a description or code

value.description: The label value when an expression condition is tripped

placeholder.description: Whether this is a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is used to define a set of endorsements, as a string, that can be applied to a quote. An endorsement is like a tag or flag that gets returned with the quote.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

def.description: The default array of endorsements if the expression can not resolve a value

name.description: The text for the form label for this property

exp.description: This is a javascript expression that is used to evaluate a response value.

description.description: This can be used to provide a description or code

value.description: The label value when an expression condition is tripped

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is used to calculate a numeric value. It uses Math.js for formula calculations.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

def.description: The default numeric value of the calculation if the formula can not resolve a value

formula.description: The formula to evaluate the result value

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step is used to call an external API and have the response data mapped via formats to step outputs.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

url.description: The endpoint URL of the external API

method.description: The endpoint method of the external API

timeout.description: The alloted timeout in milliseconds for the external API

headers.description: The custom headers for the external API. e.g. auth creds

data.description: The custom payload data for the external API.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step, like modules, is used to modularize the project, allowing for dynamic loading of modules based on the project reference and version instead of embedding

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

oneOf.description: The factor step is a auto formatted response that is like guide railed collection. The formatted response and data set schema are always the same. The factors work up to 3 dimensionality/interaction.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

result.description: The schema is for the final price that the engine will serve. This is a calculation step that has access to all created output properties.

key.description: The key property name

formula.description: The formula to evaluate the result value

type.description: The data type of the formatted value

def.description: The default value it the expression can not resolve a value

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: This step used a dataset/collection and formatted collection queries to return output values. This functions like a lookup table.

key.description: The unique step property key i.e. the key that will appear in the output

label.description: The text for the form label for this linked property

type.description: The data type of the linked value

def.description: The default value the link will resolve too.

collection.description: The collection data to query. Collection can either be a compressed array [[keys],[values]] with the first array item the keys and subsequent all values. Or it can be a normal collection of key values models.

placeholder.description: Whether this is a a placeholder step.

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

oneOf.description: The project step code schema defines a javascript code step that can be used to evaluate to a response object. This object can then have format expressions to create step outputs. If the previous step outputs an array, then that array can be accessed with "collection.<key_name>" and if the previous step outputed an object then you can use "key_name" or "inputs.<key_name>"

root_property.description: A property on the the payload that becomes the new modules payload

group.description: The group which this step belongs too

output.description: The output json schema for the quote price. Result and valid properties are required whilst optional additional properties can be defined in the format section.

exports.description: Where the output should be exported too

result.description: The schema is for the final price that the engine will serve. This is a calculation step that has access to all created output properties.

key.description: The key property name

formula.description: The formula to evaluate the result value

type.description: The data type of the formatted value

def.description: The default value it the expression can not resolve a value

valid.description: This schema defines the validity of the quote. The expression has access to custom variables of {{exclusions}} which is all the triggered exclusion rules from all exclusions steps or each exclusions step can be individually accessed as {{exclusion_key}} and combined with the or operator (||)

key.description: The key property name

exp.description: The expression to evaluate the if the quote is valid.

type.description: The data type of the valid value

def.description: The default value it the expression can not resolve a value

format.description: Defines the format of returned output properties

key.description: The key for the format

mapping.description: This is optional mapping code to a custom response with javascript.
